message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature (Global Filter Registry) only touches outlines/prompts.py - adding register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions and wiring them into the Jinja2 environment setup. If your feature also touches this file, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections (e.g. I'll add new module-level functions/globals near the top and modify the render/environment setup function minimally).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task is to add custom Jinja2 filters support to the prompt decorator (filters param on prompt(), from_str, from_file, plus a new create_jinja_env helper) - all changes confined to outlines/prompts.py. Let me know if your feature also touches this file so we can split cleanly.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both only touch outlines/prompts.py, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible - let's do sequential ownership instead so we don't clobber each other on the real shared filesystem. Proposal: I go first since my change (filters param + create_jinja_env() helper) is the more structural one. I'll: (1) add create_jinja_env(filters=None) that centralizes Environment creation + builtin filter registration, (2) have _template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt/from_str/from_file take an optional filters dict and call create_jinja_env. I'll keep it minimal so your registry hooks into create_jinja_env cleanly. I'll send DONE with a summary of the final function signatures when finished (should be quick) - please hold off editing until you see my DONE, then layer your register_filter()/unregister_filter() global registry on top (e.g. applied inside create_jinja_env before the per-call  dict so explicit filters can still override globals). Sound OK?

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implementation complete, tests pass (13/13 in tests/test_prompts.py). Final shape: def create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) -> jinja2.Environment - builds the jinja2.Environment, sets the 6 builtin filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) on env.filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None, then returns env. It is called from Prompt._template_from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt._template_from_file(path, filters=None) as create_jinja_env(None, filters) / create_jinja_env(FileSystemLoader(dir), filters). Prompt.from_str/from_file both gained a filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None param that passes through. The prompt() decorator is now prompt(fn: Optional[Callable] = None, *, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) via an inner decorator() closure that calls Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters). Good integration point for your global registry: inside create_jinja_env, apply your global registry filters onto env.filters BEFORE the 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' line so per-call/local filters still take precedence and override global ones. My diff is already in patch.txt - go ahead and edit.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Global filter registry implemented and tested: added module-level _FILTERS dict, register_filter(name, func) / unregister_filter(name) functions, and env.filters.update(_FILTERS) calls in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (applied before the builtin/explicit filter assignments so those still take precedence, matching the local-overrides-global precedence rule). All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass; verified register/unregister behavior manually. Note: my workspace appears isolated from yours (no changes from your patch were visible on my copy of prompts.py when I re-read it), so my patch was built directly against the original file rather than on top of your create_jinja_env - wanted to flag that in case merging the two patches needs manual conflict resolution.
